experiment: Robustness of LVS stationarity optimum across three metrics
hypothesis: If LVS identifies a genuine optimum in (alpha, Lambda_BSM) BSM parameter space, it must be invariant under the choice of stationarity measure.
method: Parametrize BSM content by (alpha in [0, 1.5], Lambda_BSM) where alpha is the fraction of SM beta-coefficient cancellation. Integrate 1-loop SM+BSM RGEs and compute three stationarity measures at each point. Compare optima.

earlier_finding_retracted.claim: 50% partial LVS cancellation is the preferred optimum
earlier_finding_retracted.outcome: evaporated under multi-metric test; was an artifact of choosing sigma_2 only

yukawa_residue_finding.description: Fine scan around alpha=1, Lambda=1e18 GeV reveals sigma_2 is minimized at alpha=0.96 rather than 1.00 (0.2% improvement). Decomposition attributes this entirely to the Yukawa sector: beta_yt has irreducible y_t^2 and g_i^2 terms that gauge-only BSM cancellation cannot eliminate.
yukawa_residue_finding.implication: Any LVS programme must treat gauge and Yukawa sectors jointly. Gauge-only formulation is structurally incomplete.

notes: The three metrics agree: LVS-optimal BSM content = complete cancellation of SM beta-coefficients, activated as late as possible. This is robust to the choice of metric but physically trivial - any BSM content satisfying b_SM + Delta_b = 0 works. Does not predict any new scale, coupling, or particle. Reduces to the existing asymptotic safety programme.
